rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks in valid scopes
Use visible identifiers from the target component or implementation when available
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the target component or implementation
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when inserting annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the target component or implementation when available
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
